summary: End-to-end test of Marketing CRM Hub — backend CRUD endpoints and frontend flows (Add Client, Client Drawer, Log Interaction, re-sort on last_contacted_date). Also regression-checked that all other admin pages (Overview, Talents, Applications, Projects, Links, Feedback) still load after AdminLayout sidebar gained a Marketing nav item. Everything passed.

issues: 'Add Client' button in header wraps to two lines on narrow viewports because the header title block is wide; consider shortening label to '+ Add' on <sm or using flex-wrap with min-width.

action_items: (Optional polish) Tighten the MarketingHub header layout on narrow viewports so '+ Add Client' button doesn't wrap to two lines.
(Optional polish) Marketing router file has `prefix=/api/marketing` — works, but unusual (most other routers rely on server.py-level prefix). Consider aligning convention for consistency.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend marketing router correctly serialises ObjectId via `_serialise_*` helpers — no raw `_id` leaks (good).
InteractionCreate.type is free-form string — accepts anything from 1-40 chars. Good for extensibility; frontend restricts to call/email/meeting/whatsapp, which is fine.
Client bump + interaction insert are sequential (not transactional). Comment in code acknowledges this trade-off for Atlas free-tier — acceptable.
AdminLayout: `data-testid=nav-marketing` is generated from label.toLowerCase() — works; but consider hardcoding testids per nav item to avoid silent breakage if labels are translated/renamed.

MarketingHub.jsx is ~520 lines with three components colocated — fine for now but approaching the 700-line split threshold; consider extracting AddClientDialog and ClientDrawer into their own files if this grows.
seed_data_creation: Created a handful of TEST_-prefixed clients and interactions via the live preview API. These are prefixed for easy cleanup; consider purging via a cleanup script if needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Marketing CRM endpoints live at /api/marketing/{clients,clients/{id},interactions,interactions/{client_id}}. Auth uses Bearer token from POST /api/auth/login. The clients list is sorted desc by last_contacted_date; logging an interaction bumps this field and the client re-surfaces to the top. MarketingHub.jsx uses adminApi (axios-like) which auto-prefixes /api, so internal calls are written as `/marketing/clients` etc.
